lyrics:
I gave away the weight I wore
The crowded room, the open door
I wandered out beyond the noise
And found a peace without a voice

No mirror, no map, no gold to chase
Just you and me and open space
The questions stilled, the list grew thin
And silence rose up from within

Abundant emptiness
A moment, not a place
It fades like morning mist
But leaves a warm embrace
And when I find it next to you
It feels like subtle grace

No need to speak, you understand
The quiet fits us hand in hand
We watch the sky, we feel the air
And everything we need is there

Abundant emptiness
A moment, not a place
It fades like morning mist
But leaves a warm embrace
And when I find it next to you
It feels like subtle grace

It will pass, I know it must
Like phantoms fading into mist
No center, no shell-just sky and sand
And something more than I had planned

Abundant emptiness
Still echoing in me
Not hollow, just complete
As far as eyes can see
And when I hold it next to you
It's all I'll ever need

Let it go. Let it stay
You and me and nothing else
A perfect day
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track has a warm, open texture and a reflective, ultimately uplifting emotional atmosphere, established by clean, bluesy electric guitar licks and a heartfelt lead vocal. The energy is moderate and cruising, propelled by a steady, grounded rhythm section that gives it a soulful soft-rock feel. Production is clean and spacious, creating a wide stereo image where each instrument sits clearly without the mix feeling dense. The most distinctive quality is the dynamic lift into the anthemic chorus on the lyric "Abundant emptiness," which provides a powerful emotional release from the more contemplative verses.

review.review_text: At 95 BPM in B minor, 'Abundant Emptiness' settles into a groove that feels like a long exhale, clean bluesy guitar lines carrying the verse with room to breathe before the chorus opens up into something genuinely anthemic. The lyric 'a moment, not a place' earns its weight, and PCBender delivers it with the kind of quiet conviction that makes the payoff land. Production is spacious without being sparse, and the dynamic lift into the title phrase gives the track its spine. A highlight in the catalog.
